title: TIREseq - Turbocapture Integrated RNA Expression Sequencing
author: Daniel V Brown

steps_raw.instruction: Clean all surfaces and pipettes with PCR clean wipes
Turn on a lab oven to 50 °C and another to 80 °C
Thaw frozen buffers and primers on ice
Prepare fresh 80% EtOH
Prepare TCL lysis buffer The most concentrated lysate I have made is 2,000 cells per uL. Beyond this concentration the lysate becomes too viscous from genomic DNA.
Prepare cell lysate
For adherent cells prepare enough 1x TCL to cover the surface of the culture dish. Add 1% v/v 2-Mercaptoethanol.
For suspension cells prepare an equal volume of 2x TCL as the cell culture media volume. Add 1% v/v 2-Mercaptoethanol.

For adherent cells, remove cell culture media by vacuum and add 1 volume of PBS to wash. After removing PBS, add the relevant volume of 1x TCL buffer based on estimate cell number. Use a cell scraper and p1000 pipette to homogenize lysate.
For suspension cells, add the relevant volume of 2x TCL buffer directly to culture media. Use a p1000 pipette to homogenize lysate.
The lysate may be stored for at least 6 months at -80 °C without any loss in performance.
Thaw the cell lysates On ice

Transfer 20 µL, cell lysate to a TurboCapture plate. I use a 96w pipetting robot for this step. Avoid creating bubbles or foam. A maximum of 80uL cell lysate may be used but be aware an equal volume of Kappa HiFi polymerase should be added at a later step driving up costs.
Cover the plate with a plastic seal and incubate at Room temperature for 01:00:00, on a laboratory rocker
Remove the liquid from the TurboCapture plate by flicking over a sink. Use the ELISA flicking technique. Dilute out the liquid in the sink by running the tap.

Wash the TurboCapture plate using 100 μl Buffer TCW per well. Flick the TCW into the sink Pipetting accuracy not critical here. Prepare the reverse transcriptase mix before the final wash so the TurboCapture plate does not dry out.
go to step #12 repeat the wash an additional 2 more times for a total of 3 washes

Prepare reverse transcriptase mixture: A B C D 1 Reagent 1 rxn 96 rxn + 20% Conc 2 5x Maxima buffer 4 460.8 1x 3 100mM dNTPs 0.2 23 1mM 4 100uM TIRE_TSO 0.2 23 1uM 5 20U/uL SuperaseIN 0.2 23 0.2U/uL 6 200U/uL Maxima reverse transcriptase 2 230.4 100U 7 Water 13.4 1543.7 8 Total 20 2304
Remove all buffer TCW from the TurboCapture plate by pulse centrifugation and aspirating with a multi channel pipette.
Add 20 µL, reverse transcription mix Incubate in a thermocycler for: 50 °C, 1 hour 80 °C, 10min 4 °C, Hold
Add 150 µL 100 millimolar (mM), NaOH to the plate with reverse transcription mixture and immediately flick out the liquid over the sink.
Add 100 µL 100 millimolar (mM), NaOH to the plate. Incubate for 00:05:00, room temp
Wash with 150 µL 10 millimolar (mM), Tris pH 7.5 Flick out the liquid over the sink.
go to step #19 Repeat wash for a total of 2 washes Have Kappa HiFi and the well barcoding primers thawed and ready so the plate does not dry out
Remove all Tris buffer from the TurboCapture plate by pulse centrifugation and aspirating with a multi channel pipette.
Dispense 10 µL, 2x Kappa HiFi Polymerase into each well with a repeat dispenser or multichannel pipet.
Dispense 10 µL of 1 micromolar (µM), barcoding primer to each well. Mix well. I use a 96w pipetting robot for this step.

Incubate in a thermocycler: A B C D 1 Step Temperature Time Cycles 2 Inital denaturation 98 2min 1 3 Denature 98 20sec 10 cycles 4 Anneal 60 30sec 5 Extension 72 30sec 6 Final elongation 72 2min 1 7 Hold Linear amplification protocol. Sample can be held overnight.
Prepare SPRI beads by diluting SPRIselect commerical beads 1:4 in SPRI buffer. Prepare 2.5 mL diluted SPRI beads per 96 well plate.
Pool all wells together. You can invert the plate into a SBS reservoir and centrifuge. Or use a multichannel pipette.
After pooling, mix the sample well in a single tube then distribute across 4x 1.5mL eppendorf tubes. Measure the volume of each tube with a p1000 pipette. Use the dialing down method.
Perform a 1.2x SPRI bead : sample cleanup. For example for 450uL of sample add 540 µL, diluted SPRI beads.
Incubate for 00:05:00, at room temp
Incubate on a magnet for 00:05:00 at room temp
Keep tubes on magent and remove the supernatant carefully. Add 500 µL 80% ethanol
go to step #31 for a total of 2 ethanol washes.
After removing supernatant perform a pulse centrifuge and remove all residual ethanol with a pipet
Incubate on the magnet for 00:02:00
Elute the sample with 30 µL, water Transfer 28 µL supernatant of each of the 4 aliquots into separate positions of a 0.2mL strip tube. Sample can be stored for several weeks at -20 °C

Keep half the sample at -20 °C as a backup. Prepare the following random hexamer master mix: A B C 1 Reagent 1 rxn 4 rxn + 10% 2 10x NEB buffer 3.1 2 8.8 3 100uM TIRE hexamer primer 2 8.8 4 10mM dNTPs 2 8.8 5 Linear amp product 13 6 Total 19 83.6
Add random hexamer mix to the samples and incubate 95 °C, for 2 min in a thermocycler.
Remove from thermocycler and allow the sample to cool to room temperature. Add 1 µL, Large (Klenow) Fragment and mix well.
Incubate at 25 °C, for 30min
Incubate at 75 °C, for 20min
Perform a 1.8x SPRI bead cleanup with undiluted beads with the sample protocol as go to step #29
Elute in 20 µL, water in a 0.2mL strip tube.

Prepare a PCR master mix for each of the 4 aliquots from the Klenow reaction: A B C D 1 Reagent 1 rxn 4 rxn + 10% Conc 2 Purified Klenow reaction 20 3 2x Kappa HiFi polymerase 25 110 4 10uM each TIRE PCR1 primer premix 5 22 400nM 5 Total 50 220 PCR1 master mix
Add 30 µL, PCR1 master mix to each purified Klenow reaction.

Run the following PCR protocol: %s %s *I have performed experiments comparing PCR cycle numbers in the PCR1 and PCR2 steps. 10 PCR cycles has worked well for 1 million - 10 million cells across the 96w plate For 100,000 cells total I use 12 PCR cycles. I found it better to err on the side of more PCR cycles in the 1st PCR step and less in the 2nd PCR step.
Perform a 1x SPRI bead cleanup with undiluted beads following the protocol in go to step #29
Elute in 15 µL, EB and transfer 13 µL to a new tube.
Keep the 4 tubes separate for this step but use the same index primer set.
Prepare a PCR master mix for each of the 4 aliquots from the PCR 1 reaction: %s %s

Keep Run the following PCR protocol: %s %s *I have performed experiments comparing PCR cycle numbers in the PCR1 and PCR2 steps. 8 PCR cycles has worked well for 1 million - 10 million cells across the 96w plate For 100,000 cells total I use 10 PCR cycles. I found it better to err on the side of more PCR cycles in the 1st PCR step and less in the 2nd PCR step.
Perform a 2 sided SPRI bead size selection to obtain compatible fragments for Illumina sequencing. Combine all 4 aliquots into a single tube ~ 100 µL, Crude PCR2 product
Add 50 µL, (0.5x) undiluted SPRI beads to the pooled sample.
Incubate for 00:05:00, at room temp
Incubate on a magnet for 00:05:00, at room temp
Keep the supernatant! Transfer cleared supernatant to a new 1.5mL tube.
Add 25 µL, (0.8x) undiluted SPRI beads and incubate off magnet for 00:05:00
Transfer to a magnet and incubate 00:05:00
Remove and discard supernatant. Add 500 µL, 80% ethanol while the sample is still on the magnet.
Repeat the ethanol wash go to step #51.7 for a total of 2 washes.
Pulse centrifuge to collect the liquid to the bottom of the tube. Incubate on magnet with the cap open for 00:02:00
Elute in 27 µL, EB Transfer 25 µL to a new tube as a finished library.
Quantify the final library by Qubit DNA high sensitivity kit. Expect a concentration of between 1 - 20ng/uL.

The minimum sequencing requirement is: Read 1: 50nt Index 1: 8nt Index 2: 8nt Read 2: 72nt Read 1 is the transcript and can be increased if kit capacity allows. Read 2 contains the well barcode, TSO sequence and UMI. I typically begin by sequencing 2 million reads per well ~ 200M reads per 96w plate.

The fastqs may be preprocessed with your pipeline of choice. I prefer zUMIs where the parameters are: zUMI config.yaml sequence_files: file1: name: path_to_read2.fastq.gz base_definition: - BC(1-10) - UMI(28-37) file2: name: path_to_read1.fastq.gz base_definition: - cDNA(1-50)

When I have multiple plates in a run I have bclconvert write the index reads to file and concatenate fastqs from all plates into a single file: concatenate multiplate plate fastqs cat *I1_001.fastq.gz > combined_S1_I1_001.fastq.gzcat *R1_001.fastq.gz > combined_S1_R1_001.fastq.gzcat *R2_001.fastq.gz > combined_S1_R2_001.fastq.gz

I then use the index read as the first part of the well barcode: zUMI multi plate.yaml sequence_files: file1: name: path_to_index_read1.fastq.gz base_definition: - BC(1-8) file2: name: path_to_read2.fastq.gz base_definition: - BC(1-10) - UMI(28-37) file3: name: path_to_read1.fastq.gz base_definition: - cDNA(1-50)
You will need to create a well barcode whitelist by concatenating the index reads used to all well barcodes: concatenate_plate_well_barcodes.R # Read index read 1 barcodesplateBC
Example tapestation trace of finished library on D1000 tape
